title: 'I'm not a candidate': Lagarde tells Euronews as French election speculation grows
summary: Lagarde told Euronews that she is not "a candidate", while vowing to continue championing European ideals. Speculation that she could leave the ECB early to play an active role in the campaign is rife in Brussels and Paris.
imageAlt: 'I'm not a candidate': Lagarde tells Euronews as French election speculation grows
aiSummary: Lagarde told Euronews that she is not "a candidate", while vowing to continue championing European ideals. Speculation that she could leave the ECB early to play an active role in the campaign is rife in Brussels and Paris.

fullArticle:
**"**I'm not a candidate for anything, but I'm very keen that Europe is protected, that Europe is the framework within which member states operate, including France," ECB president Christine Lagarde, who is a French national, told Euronews in an exclusive interview.

Lagarde said she is monitoring the campaign ahead of a vote due April 2027, hoping that "reason will always prevail and that France will appreciate, whoever is the leader of France that it is one key member of Europe."

"Europe is the only playground with which member states, nations and even France can actually play a significant role," she added, in an interview with The Europe Conversation.

Her comments came in the wake of new developments in the French presidential election. On Tuesday, France's National Rally leader Mari...
